diplomatic agreements with countries like guatemala and mexico have effectively controlled migration however many of these agreements lack enforcement mechanisms that reliably prevent migrant flows to the us migration routes are dynamic with migrants frequently adapting to new barriers the current strategy may slow migration but does not prevent it effectively as migrants continue to circumvent local barriers asylum system bottlenecks and overload the current administrations restrictions on asylum may appear stringent but theyve arguably worsened the asylum systems backlog without meaningful reform migrants often attempt crossing illegally as asylumseeking avenues become blocked overwhelmed or increasingly difficult to navigate instead of securing the border these barriers may simply increase unauthorized entries placing additional strain on the already stretched resources at entry points deterrence messaging issues the biden administrations mixed messaging on immigration may interpretations among migrants some of whom may still see the us as open due to policy rollbacks on deportations and removals reliance on shortterm fixes rather than tackling root causes directly unraveling under future administrations suggesting an absence of longterm bipartisan solutions
